---
title: "Laurent Series"
bibliography: ../../../references.bib
---

‚Üê [Numerical Methods](../)

Source inspiration: [@mathewsSite].

## Description

The Laurent series of a function around a center $x_0$ is

$$
f(x)=\sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} a_k (x-x_0)^k,
$$

which extends Taylor series by allowing negative powers. Those negative-power terms form the principal part and capture pole-like behavior. In many smooth cases centered away from singularities, the principal part is zero and the Laurent series reduces to an ordinary Taylor expansion.

## Animations

The Laurent animation for this topic reuses the same 27 function/interval/center cases used on the Pad√© page, but draws Laurent-series truncations instead of rational Pad√© forms.

Each frame uses a two-sided truncation with both negative and positive powers at the same time,

$$
\sum_{k=-N}^{N} a_k (x-x_0)^k,
$$

with $N$ increasing frame by frame.

Each case below follows the same case ordering as the Pad√© suite (Case 01 through Case 27).

[Julia source for all cases](laurent_animations_all.jl)

### Case 01 ‚Äî $f(x)=\sqrt{x}$, $x_0=1$, $x\in[0,10.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 01.](laurent01.gif)

### Case 02 ‚Äî $f(x)=\sqrt{x}$, $x_0=4$, $x\in[0,10.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 02.](laurent02.gif)

### Case 03 ‚Äî $f(x)=\sqrt{x}$, $x_0=5$, $x\in[0,10.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 03.](laurent03.gif)

### Case 04 ‚Äî $f(x)=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1-x}}$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-1.5,1.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 04.](laurent04.gif)

### Case 05 ‚Äî $f(x)=\log(x)$, $x_0=1$, $x\in[-0.5,4.1]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 05.](laurent05.gif)

### Case 06 ‚Äî $f(x)=\log(x)$, $x_0=2$, $x\in[-0.5,4.1]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 06.](laurent06.gif)

### Case 07 ‚Äî $f(x)=\log(1+x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-1.5,1.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 07.](laurent07.gif)

### Case 08 ‚Äî $f(x)=\sin(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-3\pi,3\pi]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 08.](laurent08.gif)

### Case 09 ‚Äî $f(x)=\cos(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-3\pi,3\pi]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 09.](laurent09.gif)

### Case 10 ‚Äî $f(x)=\sin(x)$, $x_0=\pi/4$, $x\in[-7\pi/4,9\pi/4]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 10.](laurent10.gif)

### Case 11 ‚Äî $f(x)=\cos(x)$, $x_0=\pi/3$, $x\in[-5\pi/3,7\pi/3]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 11.](laurent11.gif)

### Case 12 ‚Äî $f(x)=\tan(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-\pi,\pi]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 12.](laurent12.gif)

### Case 13 ‚Äî $f(x)=e^x$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-2,3]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 13.](laurent13.gif)

### Case 14 ‚Äî $f(x)=e^{-x}\cos(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-2,4]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 14.](laurent14.gif)

### Case 15 ‚Äî $f(x)=\cosh(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-4,4]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 15.](laurent15.gif)

### Case 16 ‚Äî $f(x)=\arctan(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-2,2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 16.](laurent16.gif)

### Case 17 ‚Äî $f(x)=\arcsin(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-1.5,1.5]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 17.](laurent17.gif)

### Case 18 ‚Äî $f(x)=J_0(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-10.2,10.2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 18.](laurent18.gif)

### Case 19 ‚Äî $f(x)=J_1(x)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-10.2,10.2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 19.](laurent19.gif)

### Case 20 ‚Äî $f(x)=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}e^{-x^2/2}$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-3,3]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 20.](laurent20.gif)

### Case 21 ‚Äî $f(x)=\dfrac{1}{2}+\dfrac{1}{2}\operatorname{erf}\!\left(\dfrac{x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)$, $x_0=0$, $x\in[-3,3]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 21.](laurent21.gif)

### Case 22 ‚Äî $f(x)=\Gamma(x)$, $x_0=1$, $x\in[-0.2,5.2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 22.](laurent22.gif)

### Case 23 ‚Äî $f(x)=\Gamma(x)$, $x_0=2$, $x\in[-0.2,5.2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 23.](laurent23.gif)

### Case 24 ‚Äî $f(x)=\Gamma(x)$, $x_0=3$, $x\in[-0.2,5.2]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 24.](laurent24.gif)

### Case 25 ‚Äî $f(x)=Y_0(x)$, $x_0=10$, $x\in[0,22]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 25.](laurent25.gif)

### Case 26 ‚Äî $f(x)=Y_0(x)$, $x_0=5$, $x\in[0,22]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 26.](laurent26.gif)

### Case 27 ‚Äî $f(x)=Y_0(x)$, $x_0=2$, $x\in[0,22]$

![Laurent-series truncation animation for case 27.](laurent27.gif)
